source_pr.title: test: fix testPointGetSuite.TestIssue20692 timeout
source_pr.body:
<!-- Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #20951 <!-- REMOVE this line if no issue to close -->

### What is changed and how it works?
Reorder tk2 and tk3 to ensure tk3 will not acquire the pessimistic lock while tk2 is retiring.

### Check List <!--REMOVE the items that are not applicable-->

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- Unit test

### Release note <!-- bugfixes or new feature need a release note -->

- No release note.

symptoms: `testPointGetSuite.TestIssue20692` intermittently times out due to racy ordering between concurrent sessions competing on pessimistic locks.
failure_signature: Timeout/hang in `TestIssue20692` when the tk3 UPDATE does not deterministically block behind tk2's pessimistic lock work
root_cause_explanation: This test runs two concurrent goroutines (tk2 INSERT and tk3 UPDATE) and relies on tk3 being blocked by tk2's pessimistic lock state. The previous synchronization was timing-sensitive (sleep + ordering), so tk3 could acquire the lock too early (or not block as expected), leading to an eventual hang/timeout. This PR stabilizes it by enforcing a deterministic happens-before relationship with channels and by using a bounded `select` + `time.After(50ms)` to assert tk3 is still blocked before committing tk2.
fix_pattern: Use deterministic channel synchronization between goroutines/sessions (instead of sleep-based ordering) to ensure the intended pessimistic-lock blocking happens and avoid timeouts.

evidence: evidence: PR title: test: fix testPointGetSuite.TestIssue20692 timeout
evidence: replace WaitGroup+Sleep with channel sync + select check to ensure ordering and prevent timeout flake (planner/core/point_get_plan_test.go)
